operator_request: Run evaluation tests on the just-implemented WP-FF-004 through different adversarial lenses, remediate findings, gaps, concerns, and risks, and clean up code where needed.

basis.activation_basis: WP-FF-004 v1 is validated high-risk compatibility and serialized-report code. Fresh review must derive attacks from the integrated diff and independently test false-PASS, boundary, negative, and counterfactual cases rather than treating its recorded green tests as proof.

sources_checked.evidence: The Rust Fuzz Book recommends structure-aware mutation/generation so tests reach semantic validator paths rather than stopping at shallow parse rejection.

Current cargo-mutants guidance distinguishes executed coverage from assertions that actually detect changed behavior; focused manual mutations can expose surviving false-PASS paths without adding an unpinned tool.

Current cargo-hack supports deterministic feature and partition matrices; WP-004 has one non-shipped package and no feature surface, so the existing all-target/all-feature canonical gate remains the relevant reuse path.
OWASP recommends strict allowlisting for outbound targets and warns that complete URL validation is parser-sensitive; live canary destinations therefore require stronger proof than an https prefix.
OWASP recommends canonicalization plus allowlist validation and avoiding caller-selected filesystem paths; repository path checks must prove canonical containment rather than lexical prefixes alone.

inspected_implementation_evidence: The integrated WP-004 diff adds one 2,129-line Rust compatibility module, command routing, seven cases, twelve negative fixtures, reports, and manual instructions.
validate_profile checks schema, identity, count self-consistency, stable IDs, and digest shape but does not bind the complete generated row content to an immutable expected digest.

run_diff consumes candidate, profile, and corpus data but directly calls differential_rows without first validating the canonical profile/corpus and its base report does not attribute the candidate input hash.
base_report unconditionally emits status PASS, including the live-canary command whose individual checks and console verdict are OBSERVED.
validate_sanitized_value uses a narrow exact-key and substring set while the manual claims allowlisted placeholders and broad machine-local/secret normalization.
require_safe_input/output use lexical relative-prefix checks and have not yet proven canonical containment across links or reparse points.
field_patterns: Mutate semantically valid structured inputs so the probe reaches deep validator logic.
Bind generated and consumed artifacts to immutable versioned identities or cryptographic digests, not only self-consistent counts.
Attribute every report-producing input and keep PASS, OBSERVED, DIFFERENT, and missing-feature states mechanically distinct.
Use strict outbound destination allowlists and canonical filesystem containment at trust boundaries.
Run simplification only after scope/risk proof, then rerun the complete matrix.
reuse_opportunities: Reuse existing strict Serde structs, SHA-256 helpers, stable IDs, atomic report writer, source-state attribution, and mutation fixture runner.
Add structure-aware unit mutations directly beside private validators instead of adding a new testing dependency.
Reuse canonical compatibility commands and FF-GATE-PR-001 for final aggregate proof.
selected_approach: Execute four serial independent lenses over the actual integrated code and serialized artifacts.
First add or run probes that demonstrate a false-PASS or policy bypass, then remediate the narrow production boundary and retain a focused regression test.
Keep all changes inside the existing non-shipped Rust compatibility surface and existing fixtures unless a confirmed finding requires manual text or governance state.
rejected_options: Rerunning only the v1 tests, because that would violate the independent-check requirement.
Adding cargo-fuzz, cargo-mutants, or another dependency/tool immediately, because focused structured mutations can test the current small private surface with existing Rust and locked dependencies.
Changing the yt-dlp baseline or expanding into Ferric feature implementation, because neither is needed to close this adversarial remediation unit.
Treating report completeness as parity or live observations as deterministic proof.

risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Bind immutable versioned content and add simultaneous row/count/digest mutation probes.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: A command emits PASS while inputs are unvalidated, unattributed, or only observed.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Validate every consumed canonical artifact, hash every external input, and parameterize report verdicts.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Secret, path, or outbound URL checks are bypassed by alternate spelling or filesystem indirection.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use explicit allowlists, canonical containment, and table-driven boundary probes.
Preserve v1 acceptance IDs and rerun the full canonical matrix after simplification.

basis.validation_plan: Capture baseline, run structured manual mutations and command-level producer-consumer probes, require RED before remediation where practical, add focused regressions, run formatting/Clippy/tests, compatibility validate/replay/diff/inventory-diff, architecture-check, verify-pr, governance parse/mirror/registry/allowlist checks, and record counterfactuals plus residual uncertainty.

preserve: All six WP-FF-004 v1 acceptance requirements and the Phase 0 proof ceiling
Rust-only production boundary with yt-dlp as research/oracle input only
Seven mandatory offline planes and mechanically separate opt-in live canaries
change: Add independent adversarial regression coverage and narrow remediations for reproduced defects
Correct misleading report, integrity, sanitizer, path, or live-policy behavior if probes confirm it
Update no-context instructions only when observable command or recovery behavior changes
assumptions_to_verify: The current committed profile, corpus, and live manifests can be immutably bound without preventing deliberate versioned regeneration.
Canonical path containment can be enforced using existing std filesystem capabilities on the supported host.
No new dependency is required for complete remediation.
non_goals: New baseline generation or live-network acceptance
Cross-platform behavior not executable on this Windows host

minimum_controls: At least two independent checks not present in v1 evidence
At least two concrete code-path counterfactuals
At least one producer-consumer boundary probe and one negative-path probe
No PASS from self-consistency, omitted inputs, swallowed errors, or OBSERVED-only results
Full accepted matrix rerun after simplification
plausible_failures: Remove or replace an inventory row while updating its count and retain PASS.
Rewrite a fixture plus manifest digest or alter expected_outcome and retain PASS.
Feed a malformed canonical profile/corpus into diff and receive a completeness PASS.
Alter a candidate without leaving a report input hash.
Run live canaries whose report status is PASS despite nondeterministic OBSERVED rows.
Leak a secret or machine-local path through an unrecognized key, spelling, or path form.
Escape the declared input/output root through canonicalization differences or a filesystem link.

execution_decomposition: Run and record clean baseline tests and gates.
Execute pinned-content structured mutations and preserve every reproducing RED case.
Execute report/candidate/replay boundary probes and truth-state counterfactuals.
Execute sanitizer, path containment, live URL, malformed-input, and failure-path probes.
Remediate confirmed findings with the smallest existing-surface implementation.
Review deletion/reuse/dependency simplification and rerun the complete matrix.
Record findings, counterfactuals, residual uncertainty, closure commit, and main containment.
